title: tragacantha strobilifera
intro: Tragacantha strobilifera, also known as the goat's thorn or milk thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of liver and gallbladder disorders. The plant's seeds contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which has been shown to improve liver function, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall health. Tragacantha strobilifera is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as capsules, tablets, and teas, to support liver health and prevent liver damage.
